mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 6.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the following week as recession fears materialize.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward 60-day returns are negative in 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative forward equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 50% of tightening periods with falling industrial production, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance with t-stat < 1.5 in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the consumer price index above 3.0% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, immediately depressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 55% of high-inflation months, or the trigger fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha with p > 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production indicates demand outpacing supply capacity, creating inflationary bottlenecks that erode real earnings and produce negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power with F-stat p > 0.10 in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 per month during a post-shock recovery phase fuels consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in 50% of high-payroll months within recovery regimes, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels directly correlate with increased corporate output and revenue generation, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with industrial production changes.
